articles.title: When Punjab Pays Twice: Hidden Cost Of How State Arbitrates
articles.summary: Picture this. A contractor files a claim of about eleven crore rupees against the Punjab government. After a long arbitration, he is awarded six and a half crore — far less than what he asked for, a result the State should be relieved by. And yet, by the time interest at eighteen per cent is added on for the years before, during, and after the proceedings, the State ends up shelling out close to twenty crore rupees. The interest alone is more than twice the principal. The taxpayer pays. Quietly.
articles.raw_summary: Picture this. A contractor files a claim of about eleven crore rupees against the Punjab government. articles.summary: Despite RBI's efforts to boost rupee usage in international trade, including Special Rupee Vostro Accounts, its adoption for invoicing and settlement saw only modest growth over the past two fiscal years. articles.title: Supreme Court Refuses To Review Judgment Mandating TET, Extends Deadline For In-Service Teachers
articles.summary: The Supreme Court refused to review its earlier judgment mandating the Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) for in-service teachers. The Bench emphasized that the educational future of children cannot be compromised for the convenience of teachers. The Court also extended the deadline for teachers to comply with the requirement. The decision impacts government school teachers across India.

articles.title: The Many Pathways of PM SHRI: How States Are Shaping School Reform

It highlights the role of state governments in implementing the program and the broader policy implications for India's education system. The focus is on the pathways states are taking to align with the scheme's objectives.

articles.title: Raw material cost spike to be absorbed by govt subsidy amid West Asian conflict : GSFC
articles.summary: State-run Gujarat State Fertilizers & Chemicals (GSFC) announced that the Indian government will cover higher raw material costs through subsidies to protect profit margins on essential fertilizers like Urea and Diammonium Phosphate (DAP). The move comes amid rising input costs due to geopolitical conflicts in West Asia. The subsidy aims to stabilize fertilizer prices and ensure supply for Indian farmers. GSFC is a key player in India's fertilizer sector.
articles.raw_summary: The state-run company said government subsidies will absorb higher input costs and protect margins in key products such as Urea and Diammonium Phosphate (DAP), triggered by geopolitical tensions in West Asia.

articles.summary: China has been blocked from importing advanced chip-making machines since 2019, limiting its ability to produce cutting-edge semiconductors. Huawei is now focusing on alternative methods to maintain competitiveness amid these restrictions. The situation highlights global semiconductor supply chain challenges and geopolitical tensions over technology access.

articles.title: Assam UCC Bill proposes polygamy ban, makes live-in relationships registration a must
articles.summary: The Assam government has introduced a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill that proposes to ban polygamy and make registration of live-in relationships mandatory. The bill exempts Scheduled Tribes from its provisions. This move aims to reform personal laws and family governance in the state, with potential implications for civil liberties and social norms.
articles.raw_summary: The Assam government has introduced a Uniform Civil Code Bill to ban polygamy and make live-in relationship registration mandatory, while exempting Scheduled Tribes from its provisions.

articles.title: Sebi drops case against NDTV for disclosure lapse

This action relates to alleged disclosure failures concerning a 2018 order involving Vishvapradhan Commercial. No penalty was imposed on NDTV. articles.title: Disgorgement As A Regulatory Remedy Under SEBI Act
articles.summary: Disgorgement is a legal remedy that helps restore fairness and prevent future wrongdoing. It requires a court or regulatory authority to force a wrongdoer to give up profits or benefits gained through misconduct. Unlike compensatory damages, which are meant to repay victims for their losses, disgorgement aims to strip offenders of their unjust profits. The main idea is that no one should benefit from illegal, fraudulent, or unethical actions. In securities regulation, especially under the system

articles.title: From Meme To Movement; Constitutional Anxiety Beneath India's “Cockroach Janta Party”
articles.summary: Democracies often reveal their deepest institutional anxieties not during elections, but during moments of ridicule. The recent rise of the so-called “Cockroach Janta Party” (CJP) may initially appear to be another transient internet phenomenon driven by memes and satire. However, the extraordinary public resonance surrounding the movement indicates that it reflects something far more significant than digital humour. Beneath the irony lies a serious constitutional conversation concerning freedom

articles.title: Around 80% polling in phase-II of Himachal panchayat elections; women surpass men in turnout
articles.summary: In the second phase of Himachal Pradesh's panchayat elections, voter turnout reached 79.79%, up from 76% in 2021. Women voters surpassed men with an 82.97% turnout compared to 76.69% for men. The higher participation, especially among women, highlights trends in electoral engagement that could influence future governance or policy decisions on inclusive voting practices.
articles.raw_summary: The percentages of female and male voters stood at 82.97% and 76.69%, respectively. The poll percentage, which was 76% in the second phase of Panchayat polls in 2021, increased to 79.79% in the second phase in 2026, officials said.

articles.summary: In Bangladesh's Barind Tract, farmers are shifting from water-intensive paddy crops to fruit farming due to a worsening water crisis. The shift raises concerns about food security, rural livelihoods, and equitable access to resources. The article underscores the broader challenges of climate-induced agricultural adaptation.
articles.raw_summary: In the north-western Barind Tract, a water crisis is driving the transition, but only among those who can afford it.

A record 2.87 trillion rupees will be transferred to the federal government.

articles.title: RBI books ₹1.69 lakh crore forex gains amid record dollar sales
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India earned ₹1.69 lakh crore from foreign exchange transactions in FY26, up 52% from the previous year, as it sold a record $53.13 billion from forex reserves to support the rupee amid a 9.5% depreciation. RBI’s total foreign income rose 27% to ₹3.28 lakh crore, helped by higher returns from foreign securities, while domestic income increased 26% to ₹1 lakh crore due to higher interest on rupee securities.

articles.title: Who Owns Your Digital Assets After a Person Dies? Gandhinagar Court Says Legal Heirs Do; Directs Apple to Hand Over the Data
articles.summary: The Gandhinagar Court ruled that legal heirs inherit the right to access a deceased person's digital assets, stating that the right to privacy does not extend beyond death. The court directed Apple to provide the deceased's data to their legal heirs. This ruling addresses the legal ambiguity around digital asset inheritance and data privacy rights after death. The decision has implications for tech companies and individuals regarding data ownership and access.
articles.raw_summary: The right to privacy, being an inherently personal right, does not survive the death of the individual.  The post Who Owns Your Digital Assets After a Person Dies? Gandhinagar Court Says Legal Heirs Do; Directs Apple to Hand Over the Data appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: In Pakistan too, ethnic Bengalis face challenges with citizenship papers
articles.summary: In Pakistan, many ethnic Bengalis have been incorrectly classified as foreigners, denying them access to education, healthcare, employment, and marriage opportunities. This issue highlights broader challenges faced by minority groups in securing citizenship rights. While the article focuses on Pakistan, it may influence discussions on similar rights violations in India, particularly concerning refugee and minority policies.
articles.raw_summary: Many Pakistani Bengalis have mistakenly been classified as foreigners. This hurts their prospects for education, healthcare access, employment and marriage.

articles.title: Forex reserves fall by $7.5 billion to $681.3 billion in week ended May 22
articles.summary: The rupee fell to a record low of 96.96 on May 20. The fall in reserves also comes amid revaluation in gold reserves. Gold reserves fell by $4.5 billion to $114.7 billion, while foreign currency assets decreased by $2.8 billion to $543 billion in the week.

The issue pertains to violations of state liquor sale regulations and enforcement mechanisms.

articles.title: Hostile Witness Testimony Can Support Acquittal Too, Not Merely Conviction: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that testimony from a hostile witness can support an accused person's acquittal if it creates serious doubts about the prosecution's evidence. This clarification comes in a case where the witness turned hostile during trial. The ruling underscores that hostile witness testimony is not solely useful for convictions but can also aid in acquittals.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court clarifies that hostile witness testimony can be used to acquit an accused if it creates a serious doubt about the prosecution's evidence.

articles.title: Absence of Accused’s Name in Inquest Report Not Proof of Innocence: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the absence of an accused person's name in an inquest report does not prove their innocence. The Court clarified that inquest reports only determine the apparent cause of death and cannot weaken a prosecution case on their own. This judgment reinforces the legal principle that such reports are not conclusive evidence in criminal proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: Inquest reports only ascertain the apparent cause of death; absence of the accused’s name cannot by itself weaken the prosecution, says the Supreme Court.

articles.summary: The Indian government is ready for El Nino's impact on the upcoming kharif crop. Contingency plans are being made for affected districts. The focus is on integrated farming and achieving self-reliance in pulses and oilseeds. Efforts are underway to ensure seed availability and explore alternative crops. This proactive approach aims to secure agricultural production despite potential weather challenges.

This proactive approach aims to secure agricultural production despite potential weather challenges.

articles.title: Predator-prey relationships and habitat quality shape a tiger reserve
articles.summary: In 2009, Panna Tiger Reserve in Madhya Pradesh lost all its tigers to poaching. After reintroduction, a study found that prey availability and habitat quality are crucial for tiger population recovery. The findings underscore the need for balanced conservation strategies beyond just reintroducing carnivores. The research was published by Mongabay India.
articles.raw_summary: In 2009, Panna Tiger Reserve in Madhya Pradesh lost all its tigers to poaching. They were reintroduced a few years later and have since re-established populations. While carnivore reintroduction has been considered as an important strategy to restore ecological balance, a recent study highlights that prey abundance and habitat quality also play an essential role. The court's decision addresses employment rights and juvenile justice, setting a legal precedent for similar cases.

articles.title: Embraer takes on Lockheed, says its KC-390 is 30% cheaper, better suited for IAF than C-130J
articles.summary: Embraer has claimed its KC-390 military transport aircraft is 30% cheaper and better suited for the Indian Air Force (IAF) than Lockheed Martin’s C-130J Hercules. The KC-390 can carry up to 26 tonnes of payload, compared to the C-130J’s 19 tonnes, while Airbus’s A400M can lift 37 tonnes. The comparison highlights potential procurement implications for India’s defence acquisitions.
articles.raw_summary: Lockheed Martin’s C-130J Hercules at lower end of requirement with payload of about 19 tonnes, KC-390 near upper limit with 26 tonnes, while Airbus A 400M can lift 37 tonnes.

It also warns against turning constitutional proceedings into publicity stunts for digital campaigns.

articles.title: No girl should give up education over lack of toilets or sanitary pads: SC tells Centre
articles.summary: The Supreme Court (Justices J B Pardiwala and R Mahadevan) directed the central government to ensure that girls are not compelled to leave school because of inadequate sanitation facilities like toilets or sanitary pads. The bench emphasized that schools must provide safe and hygienic conditions to prevent girls from being pushed into domestic responsibilities. This ruling addresses a critical barrier to girls' education and highlights the state's obligation to uphold their right to schooling.
articles.raw_summary: A Bench of Justices J B Pardiwala and R Mahadevan observed that girls should not be pushed into domestic responsibilities or forced to stay at home simply because schools fail to provide safe sanitation facilities.

This raises concerns about wildlife health and conservation policies in India's protected areas.

articles.title: Rupee depreciation: What are the implications for commodities and Indian economy?
articles.summary: The Indian rupee experienced a sharp decline in 2026, nearing Rs. 97 against the US dollar before a slight recovery. While the RBI intervenes, the rupee faces a mild depreciating bias.

Anil Trigunayat.

articles.title: Right to trauma care of citizens integral part of right to life: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to trauma care is an integral part of the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court emphasized that trauma care is critical in emergencies like accidents, where victims often experience shock and helplessness. This ruling underscores the state's obligation to ensure timely medical assistance. The decision has broad implications for healthcare policy and emergency services in the country.
articles.raw_summary: The top court said that when a person suffers an accident or any similar incident which requires urgent trauma care, they usually feel shock and disorientation, a sense of helplessness, where they have to hope that those around them will somehow help them get the care that they need.

This discovery is significant for future lunar exploration and could support India’s long-term goals in space research and technology. The findings were reported by ISRO, India’s premier space agency, and may shape future policy decisions related to space exploration and international collaborations.
articles.raw_summary: Chandrayaan-2 radar observations have revealed possible underground ice deposits inside permanently shadowed craters near the Moon’s south pole, boosting future exploration prospects.

The demands reflect concerns over governance and civil liberties in the region.

articles.raw_summary: The plea was filed after Bhagwant Mann announced on social media that Punjab would launch a campaign against stray dogs, thanking the Supreme Court for permitting such action.

articles.title: Huawei plans to build 1.4nm chips without the machine that powers TSMC, Samsung, and Intel
articles.summary: Huawei has announced plans to produce 1.4nm semiconductor chips by 2031 using its new LogicFolding technology, aiming to reduce its five-year gap with leading chipmakers like TSMC. The company claims it can achieve this without relying on ASML's EUV lithography machines, which are currently banned for Huawei. This development could impact global semiconductor supply chains and India's push for domestic chip manufacturing under initiatives like the Semiconductor Mission.
articles.raw_summary: Huawei says its new LogicFolding tech can produce 1.4nm chips by 2031, narrowing a five-year gap with TSMC — without relying on ASML's banned EUV lithography machines.
